name: ecommerce-large-demo
description: Deterministic ecommerce scenario with many customers, products, orders, payments, shipments, reviews, and lifecycle updates for browsing, joins, aggregates, and temporal inspection.

steps.name: customers schema
statements: CREATE TABLE customers.customers (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, customer_code TEXT UNIQUE, email TEXT NOT NULL, full_name TEXT NOT NULL, segment TEXT NOT NULL, loyalty_tier TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL, signup_day TEXT NOT NULL)

CREATE TABLE customers.customer_addresses (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, customer_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id), address_kind TEXT NOT NULL, country_code TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT NOT NULL, city TEXT NOT NULL, postal_code TEXT NOT NULL)

steps.name: catalog schema
statements: CREATE TABLE catalog.categories (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, category_code TEXT UNIQUE, title TEXT NOT NULL, department TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL)
CREATE TABLE catalog.suppliers (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, supplier_code TEXT UNIQUE, supplier_name TEXT NOT NULL, country_code TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL)

CREATE TABLE catalog.products (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, sku TEXT UNIQUE, category_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES categories(id), supplier_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES suppliers(id), title TEXT NOT NULL, brand TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL, list_price_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, cost_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, stock_on_hand INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0)
CREATE TABLE catalog.product_tags (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, product_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id), tag TEXT NOT NULL)

steps.name: sales schema
statements: CREATE TABLE sales.orders (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, order_number TEXT UNIQUE, customer_id TEXT NOT NULL, customer_version INT, ordered_day TEXT NOT NULL, order_status TEXT NOT NULL, currency TEXT NOT NULL, subtotal_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, discount_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, shipping_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, tax_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, grand_total_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers.customers(id) AS OF customer_version)

CREATE TABLE sales.order_items (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, order_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id), product_id TEXT NOT NULL, product_version INT, sku TEXT NOT NULL, quantity INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, unit_price_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, line_total_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES catalog.products(id) AS OF product_version)

CREATE TABLE sales.payments (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, order_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id), payment_method TEXT NOT NULL, payment_status TEXT NOT NULL, amount_cents INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, captured_day TEXT NOT NULL)

steps.name: fulfillment schema
statements: CREATE TABLE fulfillment.shipments (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, order_id TEXT NOT NULL, order_version INT, warehouse_code TEXT NOT NULL, shipment_status TEXT NOT NULL, carrier_code TEXT NOT NULL, tracking_number TEXT NOT NULL, shipped_day TEXT NOT NULL, delivered_day TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '', VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES sales.orders(id) AS OF order_version)
CREATE TABLE fulfillment.shipment_events (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, shipment_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES shipments(id), event_type TEXT NOT NULL, event_day TEXT NOT NULL, event_note TEXT NOT NULL)

steps.name: reviews schema
statements: CREATE TABLE reviews.product_reviews (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, customer_id TEXT NOT NULL, customer_version INT, product_id TEXT NOT NULL, product_version INT, order_id TEXT NOT NULL, order_version INT, rating INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, review_status TEXT NOT NULL, headline TEXT NOT NULL, body TEXT NOT NULL, submitted_day TEXT NOT NULL, VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers.customers(id) AS OF customer_version, VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (product_id) REFERENCES catalog.products(id) AS OF product_version, VERSIONED FOREIGN KEY (order_id) REFERENCES sales.orders(id) AS OF order_version)

steps.name: customers entity schema
statements: CREATE ENTITY customer_entity (ROOT customers, INCLUDES customer_addresses)

steps.name: catalog entity schema
statements: CREATE ENTITY product_entity (ROOT products, INCLUDES product_tags)

steps.name: sales entity schema
statements: CREATE ENTITY order_entity (ROOT orders, INCLUDES order_items, payments)

steps.name: fulfillment entity schema
statements: CREATE ENTITY shipment_entity (ROOT shipments, INCLUDES shipment_events)

steps.name: reviews entity schema
statements: CREATE ENTITY review_entity (ROOT product_reviews)
